name: postgres
context: PostgreSQL database: queries, extensions (pgvector, PostGIS), performance tuning, replication, and backup
info: The PostgreSQL ecosystem covers relational database development, administration, and optimization. PostgreSQL is an advanced open-source relational database known for extensibility, standards compliance, and robust feature set. This tree is organized around the jobs an agent actually performs: writing and optimizing queries, installing and configuring extensions like pgvector and PostGIS, tuning performance with EXPLAIN ANALYZE and indexing strategies, setting up streaming and logical replication for high availability, and managing backup and restore workflows with pg_dump, pg_basebackup, and point-in-time recovery. The pg library is the primary Node.js driver. psql is the standard CLI.
body:
# PostgreSQL Ecosystem

PostgreSQL is a powerful, open-source relational database where agents write queries, manage extensions, tune performance, configure replication, and handle backups. It is the most advanced open-source RDBMS, with support for JSON, full-text search, geospatial data, vector embeddings, and custom types.

## Agent Jobs

The primary tasks an agent performs in this ecosystem:

- **Write queries** — SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, CTEs, window functions, parameterized queries, and transactions. See [[postgres/queries]].
- **Install extensions** — pgvector for embeddings, PostGIS for geospatial, pg_trgm for fuzzy search, and more. See [[postgres/extensions]].
- **Tune performance** — EXPLAIN ANALYZE, index strategies, connection pooling, VACUUM, and configuration tuning. See [[postgres/performance]].
- **Set up replication** — streaming replication, logical replication, failover, and high availability. See [[postgres/replication]].
- **Backup and restore** — pg_dump, pg_basebackup, point-in-time recovery with WAL archiving. See [[postgres/backup]].

## Connection Strings

PostgreSQL connection strings follow the URI format:

```
postgresql://user:password@host:port/database?sslmode=require
```

| Parameter | Default | Description |
|-----------|---------|-------------|
| host | localhost | Database server hostname |
| port | 5432 | TCP port |
| database | postgres | Database name |
| sslmode | prefer | SSL mode: disable, allow, prefer, require, verify-ca, verify-full |
| application_name | | Identifies the client in pg_stat_activity |

## psql Basics

```bash
# Connect to a database
psql postgresql://user:pass@localhost:5432/mydb

# Common meta-commands
\dt          # list tables
\d tablename # describe table
\di          # list indexes
\dx          # list extensions
\l           # list databases
\conninfo    # current connection info
\timing      # toggle query timing
\x           # toggle expanded output
```

## Libraries

- **pg (node-postgres)** — primary Node.js driver for PostgreSQL
- **pgx** — high-performance Go driver
- **psycopg** — Python PostgreSQL adapter (psycopg2 and psycopg3)
- **Prisma** — TypeScript ORM with PostgreSQL support
- **Drizzle** — lightweight TypeScript ORM
- **SQLAlchemy** — Python SQL toolkit and ORM

sections.title: Overview
sections.body: PostgreSQL is the world's most advanced open-source relational database. It supports SQL (relational), JSON (document), full-text search, geospatial (PostGIS), and vector embeddings (pgvector). It runs on all major platforms and is available on every major cloud provider — AWS RDS, Google Cloud SQL, Azure Database, Supabase, Neon, and more. This ecosystem covers the practical tasks agents perform with PostgreSQL.

sections.title: Connection strings
sections.body: Use the standard PostgreSQL URI format: `postgresql://user:password@host:port/database`. Set `sslmode=require` for production. Use `DATABASE_URL` as the environment variable name. For connection pooling, point the URI at PgBouncer (default port 6432) instead of PostgreSQL directly. See [[postgres/performance#connection-pooling]] for pooling setup.

sections.title: psql basics
sections.body: psql is the official PostgreSQL CLI. Connect with `psql $DATABASE_URL`. Use `\dt` to list tables, `\d table` to describe a table, `\di` for indexes, `\dx` for extensions. Enable `\timing` to see query execution time. Use `\x` for expanded row output. Pipe SQL files with `psql -f script.sql`. See [[postgres/queries]] for writing SQL and [[postgres/performance#explain-analyze]] for query analysis.
